title: Pseudoroegneria setulifera
taxonomy: Pseudoroegneria setulifera is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Triticeae. The genus Pseudoroegneria is closely related to the genus Roegneria and is often considered to be part of the same group. Pseudoroegneria setulifera is native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This species is often used in plant breeding programs due to its desirable traits and adaptability.

intro: Pseudoroegneria setulifera, also known as Setaria grass or Tibetan needle grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the high-altitude regions of the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseudoroegneria setulifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Tibetan medicine, Pseudoroegneria setulifera is commonly prepared into tea, tincture, or infusions to alleviate respiratory symptoms and boost immune function.
